title: File Descriptor Leak
description: An application is leaking file descriptors until the process hits ulimit, causing 'too many open files' errors on new connections.

solution.reasoning: Post-mortem: A connection handler was opening sockets and log files on every request but only closing them on the happy path. Under sustained error-rate spikes, exception branches leaked descriptors until the process hit its soft ulimit (typically 1024). New inbound connections then failed with 'Too many open files', producing a retry storm from clients. The root cause is missing finally/close semantics in the request lifecycle, not insufficient ulimit headroom. The fix is to identify the leaking PID, inspect open FD count under /proc, restart or patch the service, and confirm descriptor count stabilises. In production, add FD utilisation metrics and alert before ulimit is reached. Note: this sandbox placeholder simulates chaos via a flag file — run the final command below to pass verification until the full scenario ships.
